constraints.analysis_summary: After Round 1 (empty guess), we have no information about the secret. All colors are possible, all positions are open. We need to start guessing to gain data.

After Round 2, we have a guess of five colors with feedback that exactly two are in the secret and misplaced. No color positions are locked. We cannot yet name any specific color as present or absent. The secret contains exactly two of {red, blue, green, yellow, white}, and those two are not at the positions they were guessed. The remaining three positions could be filled with other colors or duplicates. No prior information from Round 1.

After Round 3, we know black and purple are present. Red and blue are absent. Exactly two of {green, yellow, white} are present (the third is absent). There is one correct position in Round 3 but unknown. The remaining unknowns include the identities of the two present among {green,yellow,white}, the correct position, and the nature of the fifth slot (duplicate or new color). No positions are fully locked yet.

After Round 4, we have confirmed black and purple are present; red, blue, orange are absent. Exactly two of {green, yellow, white} are present. The correct position from round 3 must be either black at pos3 or purple at pos4 (since green, yellow, white cannot be correctly placed in round 3 due to round 4 constraints). No positions are locked. The fifth slot (beyond the four distinct present colors) could be a duplicate or an unseen color. Multiple possibilities remain for which two of green/yellow/white are present.

After Round 5, we confirm that black and purple are present, exactly two of {green, yellow, white} are present, and red, blue, orange are absent. The feedback provides additional positional constraints: the correct position in Round 5 is among the present colors, and the others are misplaced. Combined with prior rounds, we have a rich set of constraints but still multiple possibilities. The secret contains five colors: black, purple, two of {green, yellow, white}, and a fifth slot that is either a duplicate of one of these or a new color not yet guessed. No positions are locked yet.

After Round 6, we have a consistent solution that satisfies all prior feedback. The secret contains white at pos0, purple at pos2, black at pos3, green at pos4, and an unknown color at pos1. The impossible colors are red, blue, orange, and yellow. The known present colors are black, purple, white, green. The only missing piece is the identity of the color at position 1.

After Round 7, we have a fully determined secret that satisfies all feedback from rounds 1-7. The secret is [white, black, yellow, purple, purple]. All other colors (red, blue, green, orange, yellow? Wait yellow is present, so correct: impossible colors are red, blue, green, orange, and any other not in {white, black, yellow, purple}). The duplicate is purple. All positions are locked. Confidence is high.

Step 4: IDENTIFY IMPOSSIBLE COLORS - No colors were guessed, so no colors can be eliminated.
Step 5: IDENTIFY UNKNOWNS - All 4 positions and all colors are unknown. We have no constraints yet.
